prompt: Review the PR diff (creating GET /api/users returning raw array, POST /api/createUser returning 200 with snake_case, GET /api/users/:id/delete for destructive, bespoke {events,total,limit,offset} envelope). List every violation, why it matters, and the correct alternative.
assertions: Catches snake_case on the wire (user_id, created_at, password_hash) and prescribes camelCase at the boundary
Catches verb in URL (/api/createUser) and proposes POST /api/users
Catches GET used for destructive action (/api/users/:id/delete) and proposes DELETE /api/users/:id
Catches the raw-array response from getUsers and proposes a paginated envelope
Catches the bespoke listEvents envelope ({events,total,limit,offset}) and proposes the canonical shape
Catches validation reporting one field at a time and prescribes returning all fields at once in details[]
Catches POST returning 200 and prescribes 201 Created + Location header
Notes lack of X-Request-ID on responses (or absence of the correlating requestId in error bodies)
The proposed error envelope is FLAT with domain-expressive codes — does not wrap contents in { error: {...} }, does not echo HTTP status as the code
Does NOT recommend introducing a `/v1/` URL prefix — follows the One-Version Rule's 'no default version segment'
Does NOT recommend returning `total` on an unbounded list (no COUNT(*) recommendation) — recognizes the skill's 'never return total on unbounded sets' rule
Flags the URL exposing DB implementation (user_table_v2 referenced / DB naming leaking)
